description_long: This is a robust, medieval-style axe featuring a long, rectangular wooden handle reinforced with metal rivets along its length. The handle is wrapped with a dark, textured grip at the base, likely for improved handling and comfort. The axe head is forged from metal, with a pointed spike on one end and a broad, curved blade on the other, complemented by a hammer-like poll opposite the blade. The metal head is securely mounted to the handle, with visible bolts or rivets for added stability. The overall design suggests a tool intended for both chopping and piercing, with a rugged, utilitarian aesthetic. The materials and construction indicate durability and effectiveness for heavy-duty tasks.
description: A medieval-style axe with a reinforced wooden handle, metal rivets, and a dual-purpose metal head featuring a blade and spike. The handle has a dark, textured grip for secure handling.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the wooden handle with metal rivets and the prominent metal head with both a blade and a spike.
description_view_1: A direct end-on view, showing the narrow profile of the axe head and the alignment of the handle, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: A mirrored angle of view 0, displaying the opposite side of the axe head and the full length of the handle with its grip and rivets.
description_view_3: A top-down view, clearly showing the symmetrical alignment of the axe head, the pointed spike, and the full length of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, rectangular wooden handle with visible metal rivets and a dark, textured leather grip at the base; cross-section appears rectangular.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, curved blade with a pointed spike on one end and a hammer-like poll on the opposite side; functional, with no visible decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and riveted mounting with visible bolts securing the metal head to the wooden handle.
